revision.comment: Auto-generated page using script written by Immanuelle, either as a placeholder redirect or to make bots recognize the page as made by Immanuelle
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Nene_Goryōnin]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

禰々(ねね、[[享禄]]元年([[1528年]])-[[天文 (元号)|天文]]12年[[1月19日 (旧暦)|1月19日|]]([[1543年]][[2月22日]]))は、[[武田信虎]]の八女。[[諏訪頼重]]の正室。 禰々は、父の武田信虎が[[諏訪氏]]と同盟を結んだため、天文9年の11月に諏訪頼重に嫁いだ。 頼重が12月9日に[[甲府]]に婿入りし、 信虎が12月17日に[[諏訪]]を訪れた。 なお、この禰々の輿入れと引きかえに、頼重が禰々より以前に迎えていた側室の、小見氏との娘の [[諏訪御料人]]が、人質として甲府に送られてきたという説がある。 禰々は天文十一年の4月4日に寅王を出産。この年は、[[諏訪大社]]の御柱祭りの年に当たる､ 壬寅年に生まれたため、この子供は寅王と名づけられた。頼重は嫡子の誕生を非常に喜び、6月11日の宮うつしの 祭りの日に、寅王を伴い、お宮参りをし、諏訪上社に時毛の馬と太刀を奉納して祝っている。 しかし、頼重は[[武田晴信]]との戦いに敗れ、天文11年の7月21日に[[東光寺]]で自害。 この年の9月10日に諏訪氏当主の座を狙った[[高遠頼継]]が挙兵し､晴信は頼継との戦いのため、 寅王を擁し[[信濃]]に出兵した。寅王と共に甲府に戻った禰々は、心痛のためか、 天文12年の1月19日に16歳で死去した。以後の寅王の消息は､不明である。

禰々(ねね、[[享禄]]元年([[1528年]])-[[天文 (元号)|天文]]12年[[1月19日 (旧暦)|1月19日|]]([[1543年]][[2月22日]]))は、[[武田信虎]]の八女。[[諏訪頼重]]の正室。 禰々は、父の武田信虎が[[諏訪氏]]と同盟を結んだため、天文9年の11月に諏訪頼重に嫁いだ。 頼重が12月9日に[[甲府]]に婿入りし、 信虎が12月17日に[[諏訪]]を訪れた。 なお、この禰々の輿入れと引きかえに、頼重が禰々より以前に迎えていた側室の、小見氏との娘の [[諏訪御料人]]が、人質として甲府に送られてきたという説がある。 禰々は天文11年の4月4日に寅王を出産。この年は、[[諏訪大社]]の御柱祭りの年に当たる､ 壬寅年に生まれたため、この子供は寅王と名づけられた。頼重は嫡子の誕生を非常に喜び、6月11日の宮うつしの 祭りの日に、寅王を伴い、お宮参りをし、諏訪上社に時毛の馬と太刀を奉納して祝っている。 しかし、頼重は[[武田晴信]]との戦いに敗れ、天文11年の7月21日に[[東光寺]]で自害。 この年の9月10日に諏訪氏当主の座を狙った[[高遠頼継]]が挙兵し､晴信は頼継との戦いのため、 寅王を擁し[[信濃]]に出兵した。寅王と共に甲府に戻った禰々は、心痛のためか、 天文12年の1月19日に16歳で死去した。以後の寅王の消息は､不明である。

'''禰々'''(ねね、[[享禄]]元年([[1528年]])-[[天文 (元号)|天文]]12年[[1月19日 (旧暦)|1月19日]]([[1543年]][[2月22日]]))は、[[武田信虎]]の八女。[[諏訪頼重]]の正室。

禰々は天文11年（[[1542年]]）4月4日に寅王を出産。この年は、[[諏訪大社]]の御柱祭りの年に当たる壬寅年に生まれたため、この子供は寅王と名づけられた。頼重は嫡子の誕生を非常に喜び、6月11日の宮うつしの祭りの日に、寅王を伴いお宮参りをし、諏訪上社に時毛の馬と太刀を奉納して祝っている。 しかし、頼重は[[武田晴信]]との戦いに敗れ、天文11年7月21日に[[東光寺]]で自害。この年の9月10日に諏訪氏当主の座を狙った[[高遠頼継]]が挙兵し、晴信は頼継との戦いのため寅王を擁し[[信濃]]に出兵した。寅王と共に甲府に戻った禰々は、心痛のためか、天文12年1月19日に16歳で死去した。以後の寅王の消息は不明である。

{{anotheruse|[[武田信虎]]の三女|[[豊臣秀吉]]の正室お禰|高台院}} '''禰々'''('''ねね'''、[[享禄]]元年([[1528年]])-[[天文 (元号)|天文]]12年[[1月19日 (旧暦)|1月19日]]([[1543年]][[2月22日]]))は、[[武田信虎]]の3女。[[諏訪頼重]]の正室。[[武田信玄]]の異母妹。 父の武田信虎が[[諏訪氏]]と同盟を結んだため、天文9年（[[1540年]]）11月に諏訪頼重に嫁いだ。 頼重が12月9日に[[甲府]]に入り、信虎が12月17日に[[諏訪]]を訪れた。 天文11年（[[1542年]]）4月4日に寅王を出産。この年は、[[諏訪大社]]の御柱祭りの年に当たる壬寅年に生まれたため、この子供は寅王と名づけられた。頼重は嫡子の誕生を非常に喜び、6月11日の遷宮の祭りの日に、寅王を伴いお宮参りをし、諏訪上社に時毛の馬と太刀を奉納して祝っている。
しかし、頼重は[[武田晴信]]との戦いに敗れ、天文11年7月21日に[[東光寺]]で自害。9月10日には諏訪氏当主の座を狙った[[高遠頼継]]が挙兵。それに対して晴信は寅王を擁し、[[諏訪]]に出兵した。寅王と共に甲府に戻った禰々は、心痛のためか、天文12年1月19日に16歳で死去した。以後の寅王の消息は不明。 [[Category:武田氏|ねね]] {{生没年|ねね|1528年|1543年}} [[zh:武田禰禰]]

{{Otheruses|[[武田信虎]]の三女|[[豊臣秀吉]]の正室|高台院}} '''禰々'''（ねね、[[享禄]]元年（[[1528年]]）- [[天文 (元号)|天文]]12年[[1月19日 (旧暦)|1月19日]]（[[1543年]][[2月22日]]））は、甲斐国国主[[武田信虎]]の3女。信濃国諏訪領主[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]の正室。[[武田信玄|武田晴信]]（信玄）の異母妹で、信玄の次妹にあたる。禰々御料人。
== 略歴 == 禰々の父である信虎と信濃国衆[[諏訪氏]]の諏訪頼満は抗争状態にあったが、天文4年（1535年）に信虎と頼満は和睦し、天文8年（1539年）には諏訪頼満が死去すると、嫡孫にあたる頼重が継承した。天文9年（[[1540年]]）11月29日には、[[政略結婚]]により諏訪頼重に嫁ぎ（『神使御頭之日記』）、頼重は同年12月9日に武田氏本拠である[[甲府]]を訪れ、12月17日には信虎が[[諏訪]]を訪れている（『神使御頭之日記』）。
天文10年（1541年）には頼重は信虎と共同で軍事行動を行っているなど両者の関係は良好であったが、同年6月に信虎は晴信（信玄）より駿河へ追放され晴信が家督を相続すると関係が悪化し<ref>晴信の家督継承前後の武田・諏訪の関係については、[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]を参照。</ref>、天文11年（[[1542年]]）4月4日には禰々との間に男子が誕生しているものの（<ref>「守屋頼真書留」）、この年は、[[諏訪大社]]の御柱祭りの年に当たる壬寅年に生まれたため、この男子は[[長岌|寅王]]と名づけられた。頼重は嫡子の誕生を喜び、6月11日の遷宮の祭りの日に寅王を伴いお宮参りをし、諏訪上社に時毛の馬と太刀を奉納して祝ったという</ref>、同年7月2日に晴信は諏訪氏庶流の[[高遠頼継]]と共同で諏訪侵攻を開始し、同年7月に武田方に降伏した頼重は甲府に連行され、同21日に[[東光寺 (山梨県)|東光寺]]で自害している。
頼重の自害後は頼継が武田方に対して敵対するが、晴信は寅王を衰退して諏訪一族を結束させ頼継らを撃退しているが、寅王の処遇は不明。禰々は寅王とともに甲府へ戻っているが、天文12年1月19日に16歳で死去している<ref>「十輪院武田家過去帳」『[[山梨県史]]』資料編6下（県外記録）所載。</ref>。

{{Otheruses|[[武田信虎]]の三女の'''禰々御料人'''|[[豊臣秀吉]]の正室の'''ねね'''|高台院}} '''禰々御料人'''（ねねごりょうにん、[[享禄]]元年（[[1528年]]）- [[天文 (元号)|天文]]12年[[1月19日 (旧暦)|1月19日]]（[[1543年]][[2月22日]]））は、甲斐国国主[[武田信虎]]の3女。信濃国諏訪領主[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]の正室。[[武田信玄]]の異母妹で、信玄の次妹にあたる。[[武田義信]]・[[武田勝頼]]（後に[[諏訪氏]]の名跡を継ぐ）は甥。
== 略歴 == 禰々御料人の父である信虎と信濃国衆諏訪氏の諏訪頼満は抗争状態にあったが、天文4年（1535年）に信虎と頼満は和睦し、天文8年（1539年）には諏訪頼満が死去すると、嫡孫にあたる頼重が継承した。天文9年（[[1540年]]）11月29日には、[[政略結婚]]により諏訪頼重に嫁ぎ（『神使御頭之日記』）、頼重は同年12月9日に武田氏本拠である[[甲府]]を訪れ、12月17日には信虎が[[諏訪]]を訪れている（『神使御頭之日記』）。
天文10年（1541年）には頼重は信虎と共同で軍事行動を行っているなど両者の関係は良好であったが、同年6月に信虎は晴信（信玄）より駿河へ追放され晴信が家督を相続すると関係が悪化し<ref>晴信の家督継承前後の武田・諏訪の関係については、[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]を参照。</ref>、天文11年（[[1542年]]）4月4日には禰々御料人との間に男子が誕生しているものの<ref>「[[守矢頼真書留]]」）、この年は、[[諏訪大社]]の[[御柱祭]]の年に当たる壬寅年に生まれたため、この男子は[[長岌|寅王]]と名づけられた。頼重は嫡子の誕生を喜び、6月11日の遷宮の祭りの日に寅王を伴いお宮参りをし、諏訪上社に時毛の馬と太刀を奉納して祝ったという</ref>、同年7月2日に晴信は諏訪氏庶流の[[高遠頼継]]と共同で諏訪侵攻を開始し、同年7月に武田方に降伏した頼重は甲府に連行され、同21日に[[東光寺 (山梨県)|東光寺]]で自害している。
頼重の自害後は頼継が武田方に対して敵対するが、晴信は寅王を推戴して諏訪一族を結束させ頼継らを撃退しているが、寅王の処遇は不明。禰々御料人は寅王とともに甲府へ戻っているが、天文12年1月19日に16歳で死去している<ref>「十輪院武田家過去帳」『[[山梨県史]]』資料編6下（県外記録）所載。</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references />

{{Otheruses|[[武田信虎]]の三女の'''禰々御料人'''|[[豊臣秀吉]]の正室の'''ねね'''|高台院}} '''禰々御料人'''（ねねごりょうにん、[[享禄]]元年（[[1528年]]） - [[天文 (元号)|天文]]12年[[1月19日 (旧暦)|1月19日]]（[[1543年]][[2月22日]]））は、[[甲斐国]]の[[戦国大名]][[武田信虎]]の三女。信濃国諏訪領主[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]の正室。[[武田信玄]]の異母妹・次妹にあたる。[[武田義信]]・[[武田勝頼]]（後に[[諏訪氏]]の名跡を継ぐ）は甥。
== 略歴 == 禰々御料人の父である信虎と信濃国衆諏訪氏の諏訪頼満は抗争状態にあったが、天文4年（[[1535年]]）に信虎と頼満は和睦し、天文8年（[[1539年]]）に諏訪頼満が死去すると、嫡孫にあたる頼重が継承した。天文9年（[[1540年]]）11月29日には、[[政略結婚]]により諏訪頼重に嫁ぎ（『神使御頭之日記』）、頼重は同年12月9日に武田氏本拠である[[甲府]]を訪れ、12月17日には信虎が[[諏訪地域|諏訪]]を訪れている（『[[神使御頭之日記]]』）。
天文10年（[[1541年]]）には頼重は信虎と共同で軍事行動を行っているなど、両者の関係は良好であったが、同年6月に信虎が晴信（信玄）より駿河へ追放され、晴信が家督を相続すると、関係が悪化する<ref>晴信の家督継承前後の武田・諏訪の関係については、[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]を参照。</ref>。天文11年（[[1542年]]）4月4日には禰々御料人との間に男子が誕生しているものの<ref>「[[守矢頼真書留]]」）、この年は、[[諏訪大社]]の[[御柱祭]]の年に当たる壬寅年に生まれたため、この男子は[[長岌|寅王]]と名づけられた。頼重は嫡子の誕生を喜び、6月11日の遷宮の祭りの日に寅王を伴いお宮参りをし、諏訪上社に時毛の馬と太刀を奉納して祝ったという</ref>、同年7月2日に晴信は諏訪氏庶流の[[高遠頼継]]と共同で諏訪侵攻を開始し、同年7月に武田方に降伏した頼重は甲府に連行され、同21日に[[東光寺 (山梨県)|東光寺]]で自害している。
頼重の自害後は、頼継が武田方に対して敵対するが、晴信は寅王を推戴して諏訪一族を結束させ、頼継らを撃退している。しかし、寅王の処遇は不明である。禰々御料人は寅王とともに甲府へ戻っているが、天文12年1月19日に16歳で死去している<ref>「十輪院武田家過去帳」『[[山梨県史]]』資料編6下（県外記録）所載。</ref>。

頼重の自害後は、頼継が武田方に対して敵対するが、晴信は寅王を推戴して諏訪一族を結束させ、頼継らを撃退している。しかし、寅王の処遇は不明である。禰々御料人は寅王とともに甲府へ戻っているが、天文12年1月19日に16歳で死去している<ref>「十輪院武田家過去帳」『[[山梨県史]]』資料編6下（県外記録）所載。</ref>。

頼重の自害後は、頼継が武田方に対して敵対するが、晴信は寅王を推戴して諏訪一族を結束させ、頼継らを撃退している。しかし、寅王の処遇は不明である。禰々御料人は寅王とともに甲府へ戻っているが、天文12年1月19日に16歳で死去している<ref>「十輪院武田家過去帳」『[[山梨県史]]』資料編6下（県外記録）所載。</ref>。

== 略歴 == 禰々御料人の父である信虎と信濃国衆諏訪氏の諏訪頼満は抗争状態にあったが、天文4年（[[1535年]]）に信虎と頼満は和睦し、天文8年（[[1539年]]）に諏訪頼満が死去すると、嫡孫にあたる頼重が継承した。天文9年（[[1540年]]）11月29日には、[[政略結婚]]により諏訪頼重に嫁ぎ（『神使御頭之日記』）、頼重は同年12月9日に武田氏本拠である[[甲府]]を訪れ、12月17日には信虎が[[諏訪地域|諏訪]]を訪れている（『[[神使御頭之日記]]』）。
天文10年（[[1541年]]）には頼重は信虎と共同で軍事行動を行っているなど、両者の関係は良好であったが、同年6月に信虎が晴信（信玄）より駿河へ追放され、晴信が家督を相続すると、関係が悪化する<ref>晴信の家督継承前後の武田・諏訪の関係については、[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]を参照。</ref>。
天文11年（[[1542年]]）4月4日には禰々御料人との間に男子が誕生しているものの<ref>「[[守矢頼真書留]]」）、この年は、[[諏訪大社]]の[[御柱祭]]の年に当たる壬寅年に生まれたため、この男子は[[長岌|寅王]]と名づけられた。頼重は嫡子の誕生を喜び、6月11日の遷宮の祭りの日に寅王を伴いお宮参りをし、諏訪上社に時毛の馬と太刀を奉納して祝ったという</ref>、同年7月2日に晴信は諏訪氏庶流の[[高遠頼継]]と共同で諏訪侵攻を開始、晴信は夫やその家族の安全を保障させると約束し、同年7月に頼重は開城させる形で武田方に降伏した。しかし、晴信は約束を反故して、頼重と頼高の兄弟は甲府に連行され、同21日に[[東光寺 (甲府市)|東光寺]]で自害においこまれてしまう。
頼重の自害後は、頼継が武田方に対して敵対するが、晴信は寅王を推戴して諏訪一族を結束させ、頼継らを撃退している。しかし、寅王の処遇は不明である。禰々御料人は寅王とともに甲府へ戻っているが、天文12年（[[1543年]]）1月19日に16歳で死去している<ref>「十輪院武田家過去帳」『[[山梨県史]]』資料編6下（県外記録）所載。</ref>。一説では、夫・義父を兄に奪われた事による 躁鬱状態の末、自害してしまったともされている。
自らの死後、自らの子である寅王は信濃侵攻の正当性を得る為の手段として利用され続けていたとされていたが、後に信玄が[[諏訪姫]]に自身の子を産ませる事にした結果、結局は邪魔者と見なされ、後に大叔父にあたる[[諏訪満隆]]が武田家に対して大反乱を起こしたのを機に処刑されてしまった。これは、身内に対する猜疑心の強くなった信玄が、いずれ祖父や父・そして母の件で寅王に報復される事を恐れた結果、起こった事ともされている。
== 関連作品 == * [[武田信玄 (NHK大河ドラマ)|武田信玄]]（[[1988年]]、[[日本放送協会|NHK]][[大河ドラマ]]、演：[[山下容莉枝]]） * [[武田信玄 (1991年のテレビドラマ)|武田信玄]]（[[1991年]]、[[TBSテレビ|TBS]]、演：[[君島十和子|吉川十和子]]） * [[風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)|風林火山]]（[[2007年]]、NHK大河ドラマ、演：[[桜井幸子]]（少女時代：[[大塚友稀]]））
== 脚注 == <references />
{{デフォルトソート:ねねこりようにん}} [[Category:戦国時代の女性 (日本)]] [[Category:武田信虎の子女]] [[Category:甲斐武田氏]] [[Category:諏訪氏]] [[Category:信濃国の人物]] [[Category:1528年生]] [[Category:1543年没]]

{{Otheruses|[[武田信虎]]の三女の'''禰々御料人'''|[[豊臣秀吉]]の正室の'''ねね'''|高台院}} '''禰々御料人'''（ねねごりょうにん、[[享禄]]元年（[[1528年]]） - [[天文 (元号)|天文]]12年[[1月19日 (旧暦)|1月19日]]（[[1543年]][[2月22日]]））は、[[甲斐国]]の[[戦国大名]][[武田信虎]]の三女。信濃国諏訪領主[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]の正室。[[武田信玄]]の異母妹・次妹にあたる。[[武田義信]]・[[武田勝頼]]（後に[[諏訪氏]]の名跡を継ぐ）は甥。
== 略歴 == 禰々御料人の父である信虎と信濃国衆諏訪氏の諏訪頼満は抗争状態にあったが、天文4年（[[1535年]]）に信虎と頼満は和睦し、天文8年（[[1539年]]）に諏訪頼満が死去すると、嫡孫にあたる頼重が継承した。天文9年（[[1540年]]）11月29日には、[[政略結婚]]により諏訪頼重に嫁ぎ（『神使御頭之日記』）、頼重は同年12月9日に武田氏本拠である[[甲府]]を訪れ、12月17日には信虎が[[諏訪地域|諏訪]]を訪れている（『[[神使御頭之日記]]』）。

頼重の自害後は、頼継が武田方に対して敵対するが、晴信は寅王を推戴して諏訪一族を結束させ、頼継らを撃退している。しかし、寅王の処遇は不明である。禰々御料人は寅王とともに甲府へ戻っているが、天文12年（[[1543年]]）1月19日に16歳で死去している<ref>「十輪院武田家過去帳」『[[山梨県史]]』資料編6下（県外記録）所載。</ref>。一説では、夫・義父を兄に奪われた事による 躁鬱状態の末、自害してしまったともされている。
自らの死後、自らの子である寅王は信濃侵攻の正当性を得る為の手段として利用され続けていたとされていたが、後に信玄が[[諏訪御寮人]]に自身の子を産ませる事にした結果、結局は邪魔者と見なされ、後に大叔父にあたる[[諏訪満隆]]が武田家に対して大反乱を起こしたのを機に処刑されてしまった。これは、身内に対する猜疑心の強くなった信玄が、いずれ祖父や父・そして母の件で寅王に報復される事を恐れた結果、起こった事ともされている。

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|[[武田信虎]]'s third daughter, '''Nene Goryōnin'''|[[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s wife, '''Nene'''|Kōdai-in}} '''Nene Goryōnin''' (禰々御料人, 1528 – February 22, 1543) was the third daughter of {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}, a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} daimyo of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}. She was the lawful wife of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, the lord of {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪地方}} in Shinano Province. She was the younger half-sister of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}}, and her nephews included {{ill|Takeda Yoshinobu|ja|武田義信}} and {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}.
== Biography == Nene Goryōnin’s father, Nobutora, was in conflict with {{ill|Suwa Yorimitsu|ja|諏訪頼満}}, the leader of the Suwa clan. However, the two made peace in 1535 (Tenbun 4). After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, succeeded him. Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}.</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|武田信玄 (NHK大河ドラマ)}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子}}) * ''{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)}}'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀}} as a young girl)
== References == <references />
{{DEFAULTSORT:Nene Goryōnin}} [[Category:Women of the Sengoku period (Japan)]] [[Category:Daughters of Takeda Nobutora]] [[Category:Takeda clan]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:People from Shinano Province]] [[Category:1528 births]] [[Category:1543 deaths]]

Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|武田信玄 (NHK大河ドラマ)}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子}}) * ''{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)}}'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀}} as a young girl)

She was the lawful wife of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, the lord of {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪地方}} in Shinano Province. revision.text:
{{Otheruses|[[武田信虎]]'s third daughter, '''Nene Goryōnin'''|[[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s wife, '''Nene'''|Kōdai-in}} '''Nene Goryōnin''' (禰々御料人, 1528 – February 22, 1543) was the third daughter of {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}, a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} daimyo of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}. She was the lawful wife of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, the lord of {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪地方}} in Shinano Province. She was the younger half-sister of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}}, and her nephews included {{ill|Takeda Yoshinobu|ja|武田義信}} and {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}.
== Biography == Nene Goryōnin’s father, Nobutora, was in conflict with {{ill|Suwa Yorimitsu|ja|諏訪頼満}}, the leader of the Suwa clan. However, the two made peace in 1535 (Tenbun 4). After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, succeeded him. Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}.</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|武田信玄 (NHK大河ドラマ)}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子}}) * ''{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)}}'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀}} as a young girl)

She was the lawful wife of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, the lord of {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪地方}} in Shinano Province. She was the younger half-sister of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}}, and her nephews included {{ill|Takeda Yoshinobu|ja|武田義信}} and {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}.

After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, succeeded him. She was the younger half-sister of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}}, and her nephews included {{ill|Takeda Yoshinobu|ja|武田義信}} and {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}.
== Biography == Nene Goryōnin’s father, Nobutora, was in conflict with {{ill|Suwa Yorimitsu|ja|諏訪頼満}}, the leader of the Suwa clan. However, the two made peace in 1535 (Tenbun 4). After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, succeeded him. Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}.</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|武田信玄 (NHK大河ドラマ)}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子}}) * ''{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)}}'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀}} as a young girl)

Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}.</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|武田信玄 (NHK大河ドラマ)}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子}}) * ''{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)}}'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀}} as a young girl)

Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}.</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|武田信玄 (NHK大河ドラマ)}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子}}) * ''{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)}}'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀}} as a young girl)

{{Otheruses|[[武田信虎]]'s third daughter, '''Nene Goryōnin'''|[[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s wife, '''Nene'''|Kōdai-in}} '''Nene Goryōnin''' (禰々御料人, 1528 – February 22, 1543) was the third daughter of {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}, a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} daimyo of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}. She was the lawful wife of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, the lord of {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪地方}} in Shinano Province. She was the younger half-sister of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|武田信玄}}, and her nephews included {{ill|Takeda Yoshinobu|ja|武田義信}} and {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}.
== Biography == Nene Goryōnin’s father, Nobutora, was in conflict with {{ill|Suwa Yorimitsu|ja|諏訪頼満}}, the leader of the Suwa clan. However, the two made peace in 1535 (Tenbun 4). After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, succeeded him. Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}.</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|武田信玄 (NHK大河ドラマ)}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子}}) * ''{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)}}'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀}} as a young girl)
== References == <references />

Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}.</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|武田信玄 (NHK大河ドラマ)}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子}}) * ''{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)}}'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀}} as a young girl)

{{Otheruses|[[武田信虎]]'s third daughter, '''Nene Goryōnin'''|[[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s wife, '''Nene'''|Kōdai-in}} '''Nene Goryōnin''' (禰々御料人, 1528 – February 22, 1543) was the third daughter of {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}, a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} daimyo of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}. She was the lawful wife of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, the lord of {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪地方}} in Shinano Province. She was the younger half-sister of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}}, and her nephews included {{ill|Takeda Yoshinobu|ja|武田義信}} and {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}.
== Biography == Nene Goryōnin’s father, Nobutora, was in conflict with {{ill|Suwa Yorimitsu|ja|諏訪頼満}}, the leader of the Suwa clan. However, the two made peace in 1535 (Tenbun 4). After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, succeeded him. Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}.</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子}}) * ''{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)}}'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀}} as a young girl)
== References == <references />

{{Otheruses|[[武田信虎]]'s third daughter, '''Nene Goryōnin'''|[[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s wife, '''Nene'''|Kōdai-in}} '''Nene Goryōnin''' (禰々御料人, 1528 – February 22, 1543) was the third daughter of {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}}, a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} daimyo of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国}}. She was the lawful wife of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, the lord of {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪地方}} in Shinano Province. She was the younger half-sister of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}}, and her nephews included {{ill|Takeda Yoshinobu|ja|武田義信}} and {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}.
== Biography == Nene Goryōnin’s father, Nobutora, was in conflict with {{ill|Suwa Yorimitsu|ja|諏訪頼満}}, the leader of the Suwa clan. However, the two made peace in 1535 (Tenbun 4). After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}, succeeded him. Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}}.</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子}}) * ''{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)}}'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀}} as a young girl)
== References == <references />

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|[[武田信虎]]'s third daughter, '''Nene Goryōnin'''|[[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s wife, '''Nene'''|Kōdai-in}} '''Nene Goryōnin''' (禰々御料人, 1528 – February 22, 1543) was the third daughter of {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|12=draft}} daimyo of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|12=draft}}. She was the lawful wife of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重|12=draft}}, the lord of {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪地方|12=draft}} in Shinano Province. She was the younger half-sister of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen|12=draft}}, and her nephews included {{ill|Takeda Yoshinobu|ja|武田義信|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼|12=draft}}.
== Biography == Nene Goryōnin’s father, Nobutora, was in conflict with {{ill|Suwa Yorimitsu|ja|諏訪頼満|12=draft}}, the leader of the Suwa clan. However, the two made peace in 1535 (Tenbun 4). After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重|12=draft}}, succeeded him. Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重|12=draft}}.</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌|12=draft}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}}. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継|12=draft}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)|12=draft}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|12=draft}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|12=draft}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|12=draft}}) * ''{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)|12=draft}}'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|12=draft}} as a young girl)
== References == <references />

On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|12=draft}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|12=draft}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|12=draft}}) * ''{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)|12=draft}}'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|12=draft}} as a young girl)

Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)|12=draft}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|12=draft}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|12=draft}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|12=draft}}) * ''{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)|12=draft}}'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|12=draft}} as a young girl)

{{Otheruses|[[武田信虎]]'s third daughter, '''Nene Goryōnin'''|[[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s wife, '''Nene'''|Kōdai-in}} '''Nene Goryōnin''' (禰々御料人, 1528 – February 22, 1543) was the third daughter of {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎|12=draft|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|12=draft|12=draft}} daimyo of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|12=draft|12=draft}}. She was the lawful wife of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重|12=draft|12=draft}}, the lord of {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪地方|12=draft|12=draft}} in Shinano Province. She was the younger half-sister of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen|12=draft|12=draft}}, and her nephews included {{ill|Takeda Yoshinobu|ja|武田義信|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Biography == Nene Goryōnin’s father, Nobutora, was in conflict with {{ill|Suwa Yorimitsu|ja|諏訪頼満|12=draft|12=draft}}, the leader of the Suwa clan. However, the two made peace in 1535 (Tenbun 4). After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重|12=draft|12=draft}}, succeeded him. Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重|12=draft|12=draft}}.</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌|12=draft|12=draft}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継|12=draft|12=draft}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)|12=draft|12=draft}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|12=draft|12=draft}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|12=draft|12=draft}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|12=draft|12=draft}}) * ''{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)|12=draft|12=draft}}'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|12=draft|12=draft}} as a young girl)

revision.comment: 46 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:禰々 up to rev 94011019
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|[[武田信虎]]の三女の'''禰々御料人'''|[[豊臣秀吉]]の正室の'''ねね'''|高台院}} '''禰々御料人'''（ねねごりょうにん、[[享禄]]元年（[[1528年]]） - [[天文 (元号)|天文]]12年[[1月19日 (旧暦)|1月19日]]（[[1543年]][[2月22日]]））は、[[甲斐国]]の[[戦国大名]][[武田信虎]]の三女。信濃国諏訪領主[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]の正室。[[武田信玄]]の異母妹・次妹にあたる。[[武田義信]]・[[武田勝頼]]（後に[[諏訪氏]]の名跡を継ぐ）は甥。
== 略歴 == 禰々御料人の父である信虎と信濃国衆諏訪氏の諏訪頼満は抗争状態にあったが、天文4年（[[1535年]]）に信虎と頼満は和睦し、天文8年（[[1539年]]）に諏訪頼満が死去すると、嫡孫にあたる頼重が継承した。天文9年（[[1540年]]）11月29日には、[[政略結婚]]により諏訪頼重に嫁ぎ（『神使御頭之日記』）、頼重は同年12月9日に武田氏本拠である[[甲府]]を訪れ、12月17日には信虎が[[諏訪地域|諏訪]]を訪れている（『[[神使御頭之日記]]』）。
天文10年（[[1541年]]）には頼重は信虎と共同で軍事行動を行っているなど、両者の関係は良好であったが、同年6月に信虎が晴信（信玄）より駿河へ追放され、晴信が家督を相続すると、関係が悪化する<ref>晴信の家督継承前後の武田・諏訪の関係については、[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]を参照。</ref>。
天文11年（[[1542年]]）4月4日には禰々御料人との間に男子が誕生しているものの<ref>「[[守矢頼真書留]]」）、この年は、[[諏訪大社]]の[[御柱祭]]の年に当たる壬寅年に生まれたため、この男子は[[長岌|寅王]]と名づけられた。頼重は嫡子の誕生を喜び、6月11日の遷宮の祭りの日に寅王を伴いお宮参りをし、諏訪上社に時毛の馬と太刀を奉納して祝ったという</ref>、同年7月2日に晴信は諏訪氏庶流の[[高遠頼継]]と共同で諏訪侵攻を開始、晴信は夫やその家族の安全を保障させると約束し、同年7月に頼重は開城させる形で武田方に降伏した。しかし、晴信は約束を反故して、頼重と頼高の兄弟は甲府に連行され、同21日に[[東光寺 (甲府市)|東光寺]]で自害においこまれてしまう。
頼重の自害後は、頼継が武田方に対して敵対するが、晴信は寅王を推戴して諏訪一族を結束させ、頼継らを撃退している。しかし、寅王の処遇は不明である。禰々御料人は寅王とともに甲府へ戻っているが、天文12年（[[1543年]]）1月19日に16歳で死去している<ref>「十輪院武田家過去帳」『[[山梨県史]]』資料編6下（県外記録）所載。</ref>。一説では、夫・義父を兄に奪われた事による 躁鬱状態の末、自害してしまったともされている。
自らの死後、自らの子である寅王は信濃侵攻の正当性を得る為の手段として利用され続けていたとされていたが、後に信玄が[[諏訪御寮人]]に自身の子を産ませる事にした結果、結局は邪魔者と見なされ、後に大叔父にあたる[[諏訪満隆]]が武田家に対して大反乱を起こしたのを機に処刑されてしまった。これは、身内に対する猜疑心の強くなった信玄が、いずれ祖父や父・そして母の件で寅王に報復される事を恐れた結果、起こった事ともされている。
== 関連作品 == * [[武田信玄 (NHK大河ドラマ)|武田信玄]]（[[1988年]]、[[日本放送協会|NHK]][[大河ドラマ]]、演：[[山下容莉枝]]） * [[武田信玄 (1991年のテレビドラマ)|武田信玄]]（[[1991年]]、[[TBSテレビ|TBS]]、演：[[君島十和子|吉川十和子]]） * [[風林火山 (NHK大河ドラマ)|風林火山]]（[[2007年]]、NHK大河ドラマ、演：[[桜井幸子]]（少女時代：[[大塚友稀]]））
== 脚注 == <references />
{{デフォルトソート:ねねこりようにん}} [[Category:戦国時代の女性 (日本)]] [[Category:武田信虎の子女]] [[Category:甲斐武田氏]] [[Category:諏訪氏]] [[Category:信濃国の人物]] [[Category:1528年生]] [[Category:1543年没]]

On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌|12=draft}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}}. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継|12=draft}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)|12=draft}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>

On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraō|qq=}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takato Yoritsugu|qq=}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|qq=}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|qq=}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yorie Yamashita|qq=}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Towako Yoshikawa|qq=}}) * ''[[:en:Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)]]'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Sachiko|qq=}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomoki Otsuka|qq=}} as a young girl)

On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraō|qq=}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takato Yoritsugu|qq=}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|qq=}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|qq=}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yorie Yamashita|qq=}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Towako Yoshikawa|qq=}}) * ''[[:en:Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)]]'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Sachiko|qq=}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomoki Otsuka|qq=}} as a young girl)

== Biography == Nene Goryōnin’s father, Nobutora, was in conflict with {{ill|Suwa Yorimitsu|ja|諏訪頼満|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yorimitsu|qq=}}, the leader of the Suwa clan. However, the two made peace in 1535 (Tenbun 4). After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], succeeded him. Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]].</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraō|qq=}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takato Yoritsugu|qq=}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|qq=}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yorie Yamashita|qq=}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Towako Yoshikawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 君島十和子}}) * ''[[:en:Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)]]'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Sachiko|qq=}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomoki Otsuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a young girl)

<!--Nene Goryōnin--><!--Nene Goryōnin--> [[Category:People of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Women of the Sengoku period]] [[Category:武田信虎の子女]] [[Category:甲斐武田氏]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[fa:ننه گوریونین]] [[zh:武田禰禰]]

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:武田信虎の子女]] into [[Category:Children of Takeda Nobutora]] via bot
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|[[:en:武田信虎|武田信虎]]'s third daughter, '''Nene Goryōnin'''|[[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s wife, '''Nene'''|Kōdai-in}} '''Nene Goryōnin''' (禰々御料人, 1528 – February 22, 1543) was the third daughter of [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]], a [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] daimyo of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. She was the lawful wife of [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], the lord of {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪地方|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} in Shinano Province. She was the younger half-sister of {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and her nephews included [[:en:Takeda Yoshinobu|Takeda Yoshinobu]] and [[:en:Takeda Katsuyori|Takeda Katsuyori]].
== Biography == Nene Goryōnin’s father, Nobutora, was in conflict with {{ill|Suwa Yorimitsu|ja|諏訪頼満|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yorimitsu|qq=}}, the leader of the Suwa clan. However, the two made peace in 1535 (Tenbun 4). After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], succeeded him. Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]].</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraō|qq=}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takato Yoritsugu|qq=}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|qq=}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yorie Yamashita|qq=}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Towako Yoshikawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 君島十和子}}) * ''[[:en:Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)]]'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Sachiko|qq=}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomoki Otsuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a young girl)
== References == <references />

== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yorie Yamashita|qq=}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Towako Yoshikawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 君島十和子}}) * ''[[:en:Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)]]'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Sachiko|qq=}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomoki Otsuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a young girl)

== Biography == Nene Goryōnin’s father, Nobutora, was in conflict with {{ill|Suwa Yorimitsu|ja|諏訪頼満|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yorimitsu|qq=}}, the leader of the Suwa clan. However, the two made peace in 1535 (Tenbun 4). After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], succeeded him. Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]].</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraō|qq=}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takato Yoritsugu|qq=}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|qq=}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yorie Yamashita|qq=}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Towako Yoshikawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 君島十和子}}) * ''[[:en:Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)]]'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Sachiko|qq=}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomoki Otsuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a young girl)

However, the two made peace in 1535 (Tenbun 4). After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], succeeded him. Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]].</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraō|qq=|1=Chōkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651975}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takato Yoritsugu|qq=|1=Takatō Yoritsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672966}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|qq=|1=Tōkō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6529301}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yorie Yamashita|qq=|1=Yorie Yamashita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465945}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Towako Yoshikawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 君島十和子|1=Towako Kimijima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416356}}) * ''[[:en:Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)]]'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Sachiko|qq=|1=Sachiko Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248736}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomoki Otsuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a young girl)

{{Infobox person|birth_date=1528|death_date={{death date|1543|2|22}}|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Suwa Yorishige|qid=Q2511179}}|father={{ill|Takeda Nobutora|qid=Q1192628}}|relatives={{ill|Takeda Shingen|qid=Q276404}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11124807]] Wikidata name: Nene [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|[[:en:武田信虎|武田信虎]]'s third daughter, '''Nene Goryōnin'''|[[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s wife, '''Nene'''|Kōdai-in}} '''Nene Goryōnin''' (禰々御料人, 1528 – February 22, 1543) was the third daughter of [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]], a [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] daimyo of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. She was the lawful wife of [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], the lord of {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪地方|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域|1=Suwa area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631849}} in Shinano Province. She was the younger half-sister of [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and her nephews included [[:en:Takeda Yoshinobu|Takeda Yoshinobu]] and [[:en:Takeda Katsuyori|Takeda Katsuyori]].
== Biography == Nene Goryōnin’s father, Nobutora, was in conflict with {{ill|Suwa Yorimitsu|ja|諏訪頼満|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yorimitsu|qq=}}, the leader of the Suwa clan. However, the two made peace in 1535 (Tenbun 4). After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], succeeded him. Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]].</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraō|qq=|1=Chōkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651975}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takato Yoritsugu|qq=|1=Takatō Yoritsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672966}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|qq=|1=Tōkō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6529301}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yorie Yamashita|qq=|1=Yorie Yamashita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465945}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Towako Yoshikawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 君島十和子|1=Towako Kimijima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416356}}) * ''[[:en:Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)]]'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Sachiko|qq=|1=Sachiko Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248736}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomoki Otsuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a young girl)

She was the lawful wife of [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], the lord of {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪地方|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域|1=Suwa area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631849}} in Shinano Province. She was the younger half-sister of [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and her nephews included [[:en:Takeda Yoshinobu|Takeda Yoshinobu]] and [[:en:Takeda Katsuyori|Takeda Katsuyori]].
== Biography == Nene Goryōnin’s father, Nobutora, was in conflict with {{ill|Suwa Yorimitsu|ja|諏訪頼満|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yorimitsu|qq=}}, the leader of the Suwa clan. However, the two made peace in 1535 (Tenbun 4). After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], succeeded him. Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]].</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraō|qq=|1=Chōkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651975}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takato Yoritsugu|qq=|1=Takatō Yoritsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672966}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|qq=|1=Tōkō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6529301}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yorie Yamashita|qq=|1=Yorie Yamashita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465945}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Towako Yoshikawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 君島十和子|1=Towako Kimijima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416356}}) * ''[[:en:Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)]]'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Sachiko|qq=|1=Sachiko Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248736}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomoki Otsuka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a young girl)

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:ننه گوریونین]][[ja:禰々]][[zh:武田禰禰]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1528|death_date={{death date|1543|2|22}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q2511179}}|father={{ill|Takeda Nobutora|WD=Q1192628}}|relatives={{ill|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11124807]] Wikidata name: Nene [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|[[:en:武田信虎|武田信虎]]'s third daughter, '''Nene Goryōnin'''|[[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s wife, '''Nene'''|Kōdai-in}} '''Nene Goryōnin''' (禰々御料人, 1528 – February 22, 1543) was the third daughter of [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]], a [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] daimyo of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. She was the lawful wife of [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], the lord of {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域|1=Suwa area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631849}} in Shinano Province. She was the younger half-sister of [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and her nephews included [[:en:Takeda Yoshinobu|Takeda Yoshinobu]] and [[:en:Takeda Katsuyori|Takeda Katsuyori]].
== Biography == Nene Goryōnin’s father, Nobutora, was in conflict with {{ill|Suwa Yorimitsu|ja|諏訪頼満|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yorimitsu|qq=}}, the leader of the Suwa clan. However, the two made peace in 1535 (Tenbun 4). After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], succeeded him. Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]].</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraō|1=Chōkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651975}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takato Yoritsugu|1=Takatō Yoritsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672966}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|1=Tōkō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6529301}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yorie Yamashita|1=Yorie Yamashita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465945}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Towako Yoshikawa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 君島十和子|1=Towako Kimijima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416356}}) * ''[[:en:Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)]]'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Sachiko|1=Sachiko Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248736}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomoki Otsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a young girl)
== References == <references />
{{translated page|ja|禰々|version=94011019|comment=Imported full ja history}}
