<!--Nene Goryōnin--><!--Nene Goryōnin--> [[Category:People of Shinano Province]] [[Category:Women of the Sengoku period]] [[Category:Children of Takeda Nobutora]] [[Category:Takeda Clan of Kai]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[fa:ننه گوریونین]] [[zh:武田禰禰]] {{wikidata link|Q11124807}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:ننه گوریونین]][[ja:禰々]][[zh:武田禰禰]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1528|death_date={{death date|1543|2|22}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q2511179}}|father={{ill|Takeda Nobutora|WD=Q1192628}}|relatives={{ill|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11124807]] Wikidata name: Nene [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|[[:en:武田信虎|武田信虎]]'s third daughter, '''Nene Goryōnin'''|[[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s wife, '''Nene'''|Kōdai-in}} '''Nene Goryōnin''' (禰々御料人, 1528 – February 22, 1543) was the third daughter of [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]], a [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] daimyo of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. She was the lawful wife of [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], the lord of {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域|1=Suwa area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631849}} in Shinano Province. She was the younger half-sister of [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and her nephews included [[:en:Takeda Yoshinobu|Takeda Yoshinobu]] and [[:en:Takeda Katsuyori|Takeda Katsuyori]].
== Biography == Nene Goryōnin’s father, Nobutora, was in conflict with {{ill|Suwa Yorimitsu|ja|諏訪頼満|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yorimitsu|qq=}}, the leader of the Suwa clan. However, the two made peace in 1535 (Tenbun 4). After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], succeeded him. Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]].</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraō|1=Chōkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651975}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takato Yoritsugu|1=Takatō Yoritsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672966}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|1=Tōkō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6529301}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yorie Yamashita|1=Yorie Yamashita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465945}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Towako Yoshikawa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 君島十和子|1=Towako Kimijima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416356}}) * ''[[:en:Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)]]'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Sachiko|1=Sachiko Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248736}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomoki Otsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a young girl)
== References == <references />

== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yorie Yamashita|1=Yorie Yamashita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465945}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Towako Yoshikawa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 君島十和子|1=Towako Kimijima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416356}}) * ''[[:en:Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)]]'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Sachiko|1=Sachiko Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248736}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomoki Otsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a young girl)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:ننه گوریونین]][[ja:禰々]][[zh:武田禰禰]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:ننه گوریونین]][[ja:禰々]][[zh:武田禰禰]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:ننه گوریونین]][[ja:禰々]][[zh:武田禰禰]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1528|death_date={{death date|1543|2|22}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q2511179}}|father={{ill|Takeda Nobutora|WD=Q1192628}}|relatives={{ill|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11124807]] Wikidata name: Nene [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|[[:en:武田信虎|武田信虎]]'s third daughter, '''Nene Goryōnin'''|[[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]'s wife, '''Nene'''|Kōdai-in}} '''Nene Goryōnin''' (禰々御料人, 1528 – February 22, 1543) was the third daughter of [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]], a [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] daimyo of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. She was the lawful wife of [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], the lord of {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域|1=Suwa area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631849}} in Shinano Province. She was the younger half-sister of [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and her nephews included [[:en:Takeda Yoshinobu|Takeda Yoshinobu]] and [[:en:Takeda Katsuyori|Takeda Katsuyori]].
== Biography == Nene Goryōnin’s father, Nobutora, was in conflict with {{ill|Suwa Yorimitsu|ja|諏訪頼満|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yorimitsu|qq=}}, the leader of the Suwa clan. However, the two made peace in 1535 (Tenbun 4). After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]], succeeded him. Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]].</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraō|1=Chōkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651975}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takato Yoritsugu|1=Takatō Yoritsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672966}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|1=Tōkō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6529301}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yorie Yamashita|1=Yorie Yamashita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465945}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Towako Yoshikawa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 君島十和子|1=Towako Kimijima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416356}}) * ''[[:en:Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)]]'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Sachiko|1=Sachiko Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248736}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomoki Otsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a young girl)

In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige}}.</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraō|1=Chōkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651975}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}}. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takato Yoritsugu|1=Takatō Yoritsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672966}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|1=Tōkō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6529301}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yorie Yamashita|1=Yorie Yamashita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465945}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Towako Yoshikawa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 君島十和子|1=Towako Kimijima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416356}}) * ''{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|en|Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|WD=Q843000|lt=Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)}}'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Sachiko|1=Sachiko Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248736}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomoki Otsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a young girl)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:ننه گوریونین]][[ja:禰々]][[zh:武田禰禰]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:ننه گوریونین]][[ja:禰々]][[zh:武田禰禰]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:ننه گوریونین]][[ja:禰々]][[zh:武田禰禰]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1528|death_date={{death date|1543|2|22}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q2511179}}|father={{ill|Takeda Nobutora|WD=Q1192628}}|relatives={{ill|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11124807]] Wikidata name: Nene [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses|[[:en:武田信虎|武田信虎]]'s third daughter, '''Nene Goryōnin'''|{{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}'s wife, '''Nene'''|Kōdai-in}} '''Nene Goryōnin''' (禰々御料人, 1528 – February 22, 1543) was the third daughter of {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|en|Takeda Nobutora|WD=Q1192628|lt=Takeda Nobutora}}, a {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} daimyo of {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province}}. She was the lawful wife of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige}}, the lord of {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域|1=Suwa area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631849}} in Shinano Province. She was the younger half-sister of [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and her nephews included {{ill|Takeda Yoshinobu|en|Takeda Yoshinobu|WD=Q837273|lt=Takeda Yoshinobu}} and {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|en|Takeda Katsuyori|WD=Q662482|lt=Takeda Katsuyori}}.
== Biography == Nene Goryōnin’s father, Nobutora, was in conflict with {{ill|Suwa Yorimitsu|ja|諏訪頼満|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yorimitsu|qq=}}, the leader of the Suwa clan. However, the two made peace in 1535 (Tenbun 4). After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige}}, succeeded him. Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige}}.</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraō|1=Chōkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651975}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}}. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takato Yoritsugu|1=Takatō Yoritsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672966}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|1=Tōkō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6529301}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yorie Yamashita|1=Yorie Yamashita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465945}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Towako Yoshikawa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 君島十和子|1=Towako Kimijima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416356}}) * ''{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|en|Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|WD=Q843000|lt=Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)}}'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Sachiko|1=Sachiko Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248736}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomoki Otsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a young girl)

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:ننه گوریونین]][[ja:禰々]][[zh:武田禰禰]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:ننه گوریونین]][[ja:禰々]][[zh:武田禰禰]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:ننه گوریونین]][[ja:禰々]][[zh:武田禰禰]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=1528|death_date={{death date|1543|2|22}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|spouse={{ill|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q2511179}}|father={{ill|Takeda Nobutora|WD=Q1192628}}|relatives={{ill|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404}}}} {{Otheruses|[[:en:武田信虎|武田信虎]]'s third daughter, '''Nene Goryōnin'''|{{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}'s wife, '''Nene'''|Kōdai-in}} '''Nene Goryōnin''' (禰々御料人, 1528 – February 22, 1543) was the third daughter of {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|en|Takeda Nobutora|WD=Q1192628|lt=Takeda Nobutora}}, a {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} daimyo of {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province}}. She was the lawful wife of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige}}, the lord of {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域|1=Suwa area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631849}} in Shinano Province. She was the younger half-sister of [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and her nephews included {{ill|Takeda Yoshinobu|en|Takeda Yoshinobu|WD=Q837273|lt=Takeda Yoshinobu}} and {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|en|Takeda Katsuyori|WD=Q662482|lt=Takeda Katsuyori}}.
== Biography == Nene Goryōnin’s father, Nobutora, was in conflict with {{ill|Suwa Yorimitsu|ja|諏訪頼満|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yorimitsu|qq=}}, the leader of the Suwa clan. However, the two made peace in 1535 (Tenbun 4). After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige}}, succeeded him. Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige}}.</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraō|1=Chōkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651975}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}}. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takato Yoritsugu|1=Takatō Yoritsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672966}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|1=Tōkō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6529301}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yorie Yamashita|1=Yorie Yamashita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465945}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Towako Yoshikawa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 君島十和子|1=Towako Kimijima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416356}}) * ''{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|en|Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|WD=Q843000|lt=Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)}}'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Sachiko|1=Sachiko Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248736}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomoki Otsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a young girl)
== References == <references />

However, the two made peace in 1535 (Tenbun 4). After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige|1=Suwa Yorishige}}, succeeded him. Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige|1=Suwa Yorishige}}.</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraō|1=Chōkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651975}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}}. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takato Yoritsugu|1=Takatoo Yoritsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672966}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|1=Tōkō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6529301}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yorie Yamashita|1=Yorie Yamashita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465945}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Towako Yoshikawa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 君島十和子|1=Towako Kimijima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416356}}) * ''{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|en|Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|WD=Q843000|lt=Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)|1=Fūrin Kazan (TV series)}}'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Sachiko|1=Sachiko Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248736}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomoki Otsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a young girl)

{{Infobox person|birth_date=1528|death_date={{death date|1543|2|22}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|spouse={{ill|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q2511179|1=Suwa Yorishige (daimyo)}}|father={{ill|Takeda Nobutora|WD=Q1192628|1=Takeda Nobutora}}|relatives={{ill|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|1=Takeda Shingen}}}} {{Otheruses|[[:en:武田信虎|武田信虎]]'s third daughter, '''Nene Goryōnin'''|{{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}'s wife, '''Nene'''|Kōdai-in}} '''Nene Goryōnin''' (禰々御料人, 1528 – February 22, 1543) was the third daughter of {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|en|Takeda Nobutora|WD=Q1192628|lt=Takeda Nobutora|1=Takeda Nobutora}}, a {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} daimyo of {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province|1=Kai Province}}. She was the lawful wife of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige|1=Suwa Yorishige}}, the lord of {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Domain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域|1=Suwa Region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631849}} in Shinano Province. She was the younger half-sister of [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and her nephews included {{ill|Takeda Yoshinobu|en|Takeda Yoshinobu|WD=Q837273|lt=Takeda Yoshinobu|1=Takeda Yoshinobu}} and {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|en|Takeda Katsuyori|WD=Q662482|lt=Takeda Katsuyori|1=Takeda Katsuyori}}.
== Biography == Nene Goryōnin’s father, Nobutora, was in conflict with {{ill|Suwa Yorimitsu|ja|諏訪頼満|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yorimitsu|qq=}}, the leader of the Suwa clan. However, the two made peace in 1535 (Tenbun 4). After Yorimitsu's death in 1539 (Tenbun 8), his grandson, {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige|1=Suwa Yorishige}}, succeeded him. Nene married Yorishige on November 29, 1540 (Tenbun 9), as part of a political alliance.<ref name="shinshi">"Diary of Shinshi Mikado no Kō" (『神使御頭之日記』)</ref>
In 1541 (Tenbun 10), Yorishige joined Nobutora in military campaigns. However, after Nobutora was exiled to Suruga by his son, Shingen, relations between the Takeda and Suwa clans soured.<ref>For details on this period, see {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige|1=Suwa Yorishige}}.</ref>
On April 4, 1542 (Tenbun 11), Nene gave birth to a son, {{ill|Toraō|ja|長岌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toraō|1=Chōkyū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11651975}}, named for the year of his birth, the Tiger year.<ref>"Moriya Yorimasa Sho Kiroku" (『守矢頼真書留』)</ref> Yorishige celebrated his son’s birth with offerings to {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}}. However, in July of the same year, Shingen began an invasion of Suwa in collaboration with {{ill|Takato Yoritsugu|ja|高遠頼継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takato Yoritsugu|1=Takatoo Yoritsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672966}}. Though Shingen promised the safety of Yorishige and his family, Yorishige surrendered Suwa Castle and was forced to commit suicide at {{ill|Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|ja|東光寺 (甲府市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkō-ji (Kofu)|1=Tōkō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6529301}} on July 21, 1542.
After Yorishige’s death, Nene returned to Kōfu with her son. On January 19, 1543 (Tenbun 12), she passed away at the young age of 16.<ref>"Takeda Family Records of Jurinin" (『十輪院武田家過去帳』), in ''Yamanashi Prefecture Historical Records'' Vol. 6.</ref> Some speculate that she took her own life due to despair over her husband’s death and her circumstances.
Toraō’s fate remains unclear, but it is believed that he was eventually executed during internal conflicts in the Takeda clan, which feared his potential for revenge.<ref name="shinshi"/>
== In Popular Culture == * ''{{ill|Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja|Takeda Shingen (NHK大河ドラマ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen (NHK Taiga drama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' (1988, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Yorie Yamashita|ja|山下容莉枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yorie Yamashita|1=Yorie Yamashita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465945}}) * ''Takeda Shingen'' (1991, TBS, portrayed by {{ill|Towako Yoshikawa|ja|吉川十和子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Towako Yoshikawa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 君島十和子|1=Towako Kimijima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416356}}) * ''{{ill|Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|en|Fūrin Kazan (TV series)|WD=Q843000|lt=Fūrin Kazan (NHK Taiga drama)|1=Fūrin Kazan (TV series)}}'' (2007, NHK Taiga Drama, portrayed by {{ill|Sakurai Sachiko|ja|桜井幸子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Sachiko|1=Sachiko Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11248736}} and {{ill|Tomoki Otsuka|ja|大塚友稀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomoki Otsuka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as a young girl)
